most_affected_files.primary_issue: Demo section (slides 3-8) contains unredacted GenServer names, tech stack details, and implementation patterns
Demo section evidence_data and Q&A answers expose ETS, GenServer, Phoenix LiveView, PubSub, and fire-and-forget pattern details
Demo architecture overview slide (slide 8) contains component lists with internal module names and tech-specific labels

key_observations: Investor narrative is clean — properly redacted at maximum level with no IP leakage detected
Partner narrative is mostly clean — a few Elixir references that are borderline acceptable at moderate level
Demo narrative is the primary offender — light redaction level was applied too liberally, exposing GenServer names, ETS, PubSub, Phoenix LiveView, SwiftUI, D3.js, and fire-and-forget patterns
Diagrams are well-abstracted — only minor warnings about fire-and-forget terminology in 3 of 5 diagrams
No file paths, credentials, project IDs, or team member names leaked anywhere in external files
The redaction-rules.json is comprehensive and would catch all these violations if consistently applied
recommended_actions: PRIORITY 1: Redact all GenServer/Store module names from narrative-content.json demo slides 3, 6, 7
PRIORITY 2: Remove all Elixir/Phoenix/ETS/OTP/SwiftUI/D3.js references from slides.json demo slide 8
PRIORITY 3: Scrub speaker-notes.json demo evidence_data fields of tech-specific implementation details
PRIORITY 4: Replace /api/register endpoint path in slides.json demo slide 3
PRIORITY 5: Replace fire-and-forget terminology in diagrams with non-blocking equivalent
PRIORITY 6: Remove port 3032 from slides.json demo slide 6 scan examples
